title: Fixed-Degree Polynomial Identity Witnesses

solver_reuse.target: fixed polynomial false-root QF_LIA/Diophantine regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Polynomial evaluation contradiction where p(x)=x^2+1 at x=1 forces p(1)=2, but the malformed root row claims p(1)=0.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional polynomial-identity rows only when they add distinct coefficient, factor, root, or exact-rational solver pressure; keep irreducibility, algebraic closure, broad factorization, and polynomial theorem schemas in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: Encode fixed-degree polynomial identities as deterministic Axeyum terms.
Replay root and factor witnesses through Axeyum model evaluation.
Keep the false rational-root row tied to checked QF_LIA/Diophantine evidence for the fixed polynomial evaluation contradiction.
Add checked proof/evidence routes for broader fixed-degree no-counterexample claims.
Keep irreducibility, algebraic closure, and broad factorization theorems marked Lean-horizon.
